scholarships.name: MEXT Japanese Government Scholarship
scholarships.provider: Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT), Japan
scholarships.hostUniversities: Japanese national, public and private universities & colleges of technology
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full tuition exemption plus a monthly stipend and round-trip international airfare for the full study period.
benefits: Full waiver of tuition, entrance exam and matriculation fees
Round-trip economy-class international airfare to and from Japan
Supplemental regional allowance in designated areas
eligibility: Nationality of a country with diplomatic relations with Japan
Meet age limits by category (e.g. Research applicants under ~35, Undergraduate under 25)
Completed required years of prior education (bachelor's/16 years for Research track)
Willingness to learn Japanese
Meet health requirements
Dual nationals must relinquish Japanese citizenship by arrival
documentsRequired: Application form and placement preference form
Certificate of Health completed by a physician
Field of study and research plan (Research track)
scholarships.deadlineNote: Embassy recommendation call opens mid-April; each Japanese Embassy/Consulate sets its own country deadline (typically April-May).
scholarships.durationNote: Bachelors/Masters/PhD, plus 6-12 months language preparatory training
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: Your local Japanese Embassy/Consulate country deadline
Valid passport and proof of nationality

scholarships.name: Global Korea Scholarship (GKS)
scholarships.provider: National Institute for International Education (NIIED), Ministry of Education, South Korea
scholarships.fundingSummary: Covers tuition, one year of Korean language training, round-trip airfare and a monthly living stipend for the full degree period.
benefits: Tuition covered (NIIED subsidy up to 5,000,000 KRW/semester; excess paid by university)
One year of Korean language training
Degree completion grant and thesis printing support
eligibility: Applicant and both parents must hold non-Korean citizenship
Age under 25 (undergraduate) or under 40 (graduate)
Cumulative GPA at or above 80% (100 scale) or top 20% of class
High school diploma (undergraduate) or Bachelor's/Master's (graduate)
Good physical and mental health
Not a previous GKS awardee at the same level
documentsRequired: Proof of citizenship for applicant and both parents
TOPIK or English proficiency certificate (if held)
scholarships.deadlineNote: Graduate track applies ~February-March; undergraduate ~September-October. Embassy-track deadlines are set by each embassy.
scholarships.durationNote: 1 year Korean language + Bachelors (4-6 yr) / Masters (2 yr) / PhD (3 yr)
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: Decide Embassy vs University track and confirm the deadline
Citizenship proof for you and both parents

scholarships.name: Turkiye Burslari (Turkiye Scholarships)
scholarships.provider: Presidency for Turks Abroad and Related Communities (YTB), Republic of Turkiye
scholarships.hostUniversities: Turkish public and some private universities nationwide (placement by the program)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Fully funded government scholarship covering tuition, a monthly stipend, accommodation, health insurance, a Turkish language course and flights.
benefits: Full tuition fees for the whole program
Once-off round-trip flight ticket (arrival and departure)
eligibility: Citizen of any country except Turkiye (and not a former Turkish citizen)
Not already enrolled in a Turkish university at the level applied for
Age under 21 (Bachelor's), under 30 (Master's), under 35 (PhD)
Minimum academic success: 70% (Bachelor's), 75% (graduate), 90% (health sciences)
Graduated or in the final year of current program
Required exam/language scores if the chosen program demands them
documentsRequired: Research proposal and writing sample (PhD only)
scholarships.deadlineNote: Application window typically opens in January and closes in late February; verify the annual application calendar.
scholarships.durationNote: 1 year language course + Bachelors (4-6 yr) / Masters (2 yr) / PhD (~4 yr)

scholarships.name: KAUST Fellowship
scholarships.provider: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST), Saudi Arabia
scholarships.hostUniversities: KAUST (single campus in Thuwal, Saudi Arabia)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Fully funded fellowship for admitted MS/PhD students covering tuition, a living stipend, housing, insurance and relocation support.
benefits: Full tuition and fees (up to ~USD 35,000/year) plus bench fees
Medical/health insurance for students and eligible family
eligibility: Open to international applicants worldwide
Strong academic record meeting program admission requirements
English proficiency (IELTS, TOEFL iBT, Pearson PTE or Cambridge CAE)
Three letters of recommendation
GRE optional (welcomed but not required)
Applicants with external full sponsorship may not qualify for the fellowship
documentsRequired: Official academic transcripts (translated if not in English)
scholarships.deadlineNote: Two intakes; Fall deadline in spring (~May), Spring deadline ~October. Applications typically open mid-August.
scholarships.durationNote: MS ~1.5 yr; PhD ~3-4+ yr; combined MS/PhD tracks available
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: CV and a target research area or faculty fit

scholarships.name: Australia Awards Scholarships
scholarships.provider: Australian Government - Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Comprehensive award covering tuition, living costs, travel and health cover for the full study period in Australia.
benefits: Return economy air travel to and from Australia
Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC) for the full duration
Introductory Academic Program before study begins
eligibility: Citizen of a participating developing/partner country
Not a citizen or permanent resident of Australia
Meet the admission requirements of the Australian institution/course
Meet English-language proficiency requirements
Satisfy home-country-specific criteria
Meet the prior-award residency rule if previously an Australia Awards holder
documentsRequired: Evidence of citizenship of a participating country
scholarships.deadlineNote: Opening/closing dates differ by country; applicants check their own country's dates in the OASIS portal (generally around April-May).
scholarships.durationNote: Minimum period to complete the program plus preparatory training (typically 1-2 yr for Masters)
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: Proof of citizenship of a participating country
A study/career plan linked to home-country development

scholarships.name: Australian Government Research Training Program (RTP)
scholarships.provider: Australian Government - Department of Education (administered by universities)
scholarships.hostUniversities: Individual Australian universities administer their own RTP allocations
scholarships.fundingSummary: Government support for research-degree students covering tuition offset, a tax-free living stipend and allowances, administered by each university.
benefits: RTP Fees Offset (tuition offset for the research degree)
Tax-free living stipend (base rate approx. AUD 37,010/year in 2026, indexed annually)
Paid sick, maternity and parental leave (university-dependent)
OSHC health-cover reimbursement for international recipients (university-dependent)
eligibility: Open to both domestic and international students
Enrolled in or admitted to a Research Doctorate or Research Masters
Awarded competitively on academic merit and research potential
Meet the university's admission and English-language requirements
Not concurrently holding an equivalent living-allowance scholarship
Commencing or early in the research degree
documentsRequired: Evidence of research capacity or publications
scholarships.deadlineNote: No single national deadline; each university sets and runs its own RTP scholarship rounds.
scholarships.intake: Varies by university (multiple rounds per year)
scholarships.durationNote: Research Doctorate 3-4 yr FT; Research Masters up to 2 yr FT
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: A supervisor and research topic/proposal aligned with the university

scholarships.name: Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships
scholarships.provider: New Zealand Government - Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade (MFAT)
scholarships.hostUniversities: New Zealand universities (e.g. Auckland, Massey, Victoria, Lincoln, Canterbury, AUT)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full scholarship covering tuition, living costs, travel, insurance and related allowances for the study period in New Zealand.
benefits: Medical insurance while in New Zealand
Return airfares (plus one home visit for programs over 1.6 years)
Research/thesis cost support and tutoring where needed
eligibility: Citizen of an eligible developing country (current cycle: Pacific and Asia)
At least 18 when signing the offer letter
Postgraduate applicants: 1 year full-time relevant work experience (some waivers)
Meet institution academic entry standards
English proficiency (postgrad generally IELTS 6.5; undergrad IELTS 6.0)
Resided in home country for 2 years before applying (some exceptions)
Commit to return and contribute to home country for at least 2 years
documentsRequired: Academic transcripts including the grading scale
Verified English-translated transcripts and completion certificates
scholarships.deadlineNote: Deadlines vary by country/region (roughly February-March); high-volume countries may close early, so apply early.
scholarships.intake: Annual (one main tertiary round per year)
scholarships.durationNote: Full length of the qualification (Masters ~1-2 yr, PhD ~3+ yr)
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: Academic transcripts with grading scale and translations
English test results / proof of proficiency
A study subject aligned with your country's priority areas

scholarships.name: Stipendium Hungaricum
scholarships.provider: Government of Hungary - Tempus Public Foundation
scholarships.hostUniversities: Around 30 Hungarian higher-education institutions offering 900+ programmes
scholarships.fundingSummary: Covers full tuition, a monthly living stipend, accommodation support and medical insurance for the whole scholarship period.
benefits: Monthly stipend HUF 43,700 for non-degree/Bachelor/Master/one-tier Master
Free dormitory place or HUF 40,000/month accommodation contribution
Medical insurance (national health service plus supplementary up to HUF 65,000/year)
eligibility: Citizen of an eligible sending partner country
Apply through the home country's responsible authority
Meet the host institution's academic entry requirements
Meet language proficiency set by the host institution
Hungarian-language studies require B2 or a 1-year preparatory course
Not born after 31 August 2008 (minimum age threshold; Dance exempt)
documentsRequired: Completed online application form with recent photo
Proof of language proficiency with translation
School certificates and transcripts with translations
Copy of passport or national ID
scholarships.deadlineNote: Single firm deadline in mid-January (2026/27: 15 January 2026); some sending countries set earlier national deadlines.
scholarships.durationNote: Bachelor ~3-4 yr, Master ~2 yr, PhD ~4 yr (8 semesters, two phases)
